input.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Smart daily work session manager
# Usage: git done "description of today's work"

if [ $# -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Error: Please provide a description of today's work"
    echo "Usage: git done \"fixed login bug and added user profile\""
    exit 1
fi

# Get current date and time
DATE=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d")
TIME=$(date +"%H:%M:%S")
TIMESTAMP="$DATE $TIME"

# Create daily work log directory
mkdir -p .git/daily-logs

# Create today's work log entry
LOG_FILE=".git/daily-logs/$DATE.md"

# Add entry to today's log
cat >> "$LOG_FILE" << EOF

## Work Session - $TIME
**Description**: $1
**Files Changed**:
$(git status --porcelain | sed 's/^/ - /')

**Git Status**: $(git status --porcelain | wc -l) files modified

---

EOF

# Add all changes
git add .

# Commit with detailed message
COMMIT_MSG="[$DATE] $1"

git commit -m "$COMMIT_MSG"

# Push if remote exists
if git remote get-url origin &>/dev/null; then
    git push origin $(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
    echo "✓ Work session saved and pushed to remote repository!"
else
    echo "✓ Work session saved locally!"
    echo "ℹ No remote repository found. Set up with: git remote add origin <url>"
fi

# Show summary
echo ""
echo "📊 Today's Work Summary:"
echo "📅 Date: $DATE"
echo "⏰ Time: $TIME"
echo "📝 Description: $1"
echo "📁 Log file: $LOG_FILE"
echo ""
echo "💡 View full daily log with: cat $LOG_FILE"
